name: Retro Gaming Classics
tagline: Example fan board, submit yours with a share link from the Board Builder!

questions.q: This mustachioed plumber first stomped a barrel throwing ape in 1981.
Sega's blue blur, built for speed and chili dogs.
This pink puffball inhales enemies and steals their powers.
Capcom's blue bomber, who defeats Robot Masters and takes their weapons.
This bandicoot spun his way onto the original PlayStation in 1996.
The 1985 Nintendo console that revived home gaming in the West.
questions.a: What is the NES (Nintendo Entertainment System)?

questions.q: Sony's first game console, launched in 1994.
This Sega console's add ons included a CD drive and the 32X.
Nintendo's 1996 console introduced the analog stick to its controllers.
Sega's final home console, beloved and discontinued in 2001.
The classic fix attempted on misbehaving cartridges, despite officially being a bad idea.
questions.a: What is blowing into the cartridge?

questions.q: This 1986 NES adventure introduced battery backed saves so you could shut off the console without losing Hyrule.
questions.a: What is The Legend of Zelda?

questions.q: The infamous cheat code most associated with Konami games.
questions.a: What is the Konami Code (up, up, down, down, left, right, left, right, B, A)?

questions.q: This Game Boy title was bundled with the handheld in the West and made falling blocks a global obsession.
The gold NES cartridge was a Zelda trademark. this other Nintendo franchise also shipped a gold cart for its second NES entry.
questions.a: What is Zelda II, The Adventure of Link? (Accept, it was still Zelda, the trick question everyone falls for.)

questions.q: This 1980 maze chase starred a yellow circle with an appetite.
Aliens descend in rows in this 1978 shooter that popularized the high score.
This 1991 Capcom fighter gave the world Hadoukens and quarter circle inputs.
The four player 1987 beat em up where you picked Leonardo, Raphael, Michelangelo, or Donatello... in the sewer.
questions.a: What is Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (the arcade game)?

questions.q: This 1981 Williams shooter about rescuing astronauts is famed as one of the hardest arcade games ever.
In classic fighting games, the announcer starts every match with 'Round one...'
The Mortal Kombat announcer's famous instruction when your opponent is dazed.
Zero Wing's legendarily mistranslated line about the ownership of bases.
questions.a: What is 'All your base are belong to us'?

questions.q: The arcade plea that appears when your fighter falls and the timer counts down from 10.
The Oregon Trail's most famous cause of death, now a t shirt industry.
questions.a: What is dysentery ('You have died of dysentery')?

final.q: This 1972 Atari table tennis game is widely credited with launching the commercial video game industry.
